title: Electric Bike/Train Coastal Tour
description: Want to ride the rails and explore on your electric or road bike? The tour begins at the Solana Beach Train Station where you will board the train, with your bike, headed to Carlsbad Village. The train fare of $5 is included in your tour charge. The train follows the coastline, so you can enjoy spectacular views along the way. Once off the train in Carlsbad, your bike adventure will begin. As you ride back to Solana Beach you will enjoy incredible coastline vistas, white sand beaches, meditation gardens, local history, and restaurants to experience for lunch. The bike riding leg of the tour is about 16 miles with up to 14 stops along the way. This tour is a cell phone, narrated and guided tour app. The map on your phone will show you the stops and the route we recommend. It will also show your position along the route, so you won't get lost. When you get close to a scheduled stop, your app tour guide will share information, history, and stories with you. Do not forget your ear buds or earphones for the best audio enjoyment. If you need to rent an electric bike for the tour please contact Pedego Solana Beach at 858.764.2979 or you can make an online reservation at pedegosolanabeach.com. Their rental bikes come with a cell phone holder on the handlebar and a standard USB port to keep your phone charged from the bike's power source. Bring your phone charging cord to connect with the bike. The bike rental is not included in the tour cost. This experience should not be rushed, so plan to start in the morning and enjoy the entire day. Fun train ride, incredible coastline views, tasty food, no traffic or parking hassles, and fun in the sun. Most of the tour you will be riding in a bike lane on a road with automobiles.

title: Resident Free Thursdays!
description: Starting in 2026, local San Diego residents can visit the museum and take a self-guided tour of the museum for FREE every second Thursday of the month during our standard operating hours. Guests must show a photo ID with a San Diego address to qualify for free admission. Every 2nd Thursday of the month from 10:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. admission will be free with a photo ID with San Diego address Redeem At the ticket window of the Gaslamp Museum at the Davis-Horton House 410 Island Ave, San Diego, CA, 92101

title: La Jolla Tour - Sip, Savor and Sea
description: The Sip, Savor and Sea tour is a great way to explore the coastal town of La Jolla with a professional tour guide. La Jolla is known as the "Jewel of San Diego" with some much marine wildlife, art, and historic figures who have paved the way in the world of science and medicine. Learn about their stories, enjoy seaside views, and walk along the village streets lined with galleries and trendy restaurants. What to Expect: A guided walking tour of La Jolla Village & Cove, weaving together scenic oceanfront views, local history, culinary tastings, and art. The walk covers roughly ½ to ¾ mile, including a downhill segment to reach the Cove—so comfy shoes are a must. Culinary & Culinary Highlights: Enjoy a savory food and drink tasting at a local restaurant, often featuring gourmet tacos and margaritas—Puesto is one favored stop. Wrap up on a sweet note with dessert, typically artisanal gelato from a local French/Italian-style bakery. Cultural and Scenic Stops: Visit an art gallery, led by a curator, offering a glimpse into the local contemporary art scene. Stroll past iconic landmarks like Sunny Jim's Sea Cave, a quirky and historic cave accessed via the famous Cave Store. At the picturesque La Jolla Cove, spot sea lions basking on rocks and take in sweeping ocean views. Storytelling & Local Flavor: Guides share engaging stories about La Jolla's history, with a special nod to pioneering figures—especially women—who helped shape the area's rich cultural and scientific landscape. You'll also hear fun local anecdotes—about Hollywood-era hangouts, marine mysteries, and more.

title: Cin! Cin! (Cheers!): Wine Tour
description: A guided tour through the flavors and aromas of Little Italy. Planning a romantic date or your next friend's night out? Our Little Italy wine tasting tour is the perfect way to elevate your outing and get to know one of San Diego's most unique downtown neighborhoods. As we stroll through this historic fishing town, you'll learn all about the area's rich history and, of course, sample the food and award winning wine that makes Little Italy one of the best dining locations in all of San Diego and, dare we say, Southern California. About Wine Tasting In Little ItalyVino! Vino! is a walking San Diego wine tour and a great way to get to know Little Italy, San Diego - one of the chicest neighborhoods in the Downtown area. Meet with your tour guide and head on to your culinary flavor adventure. With many unique boutiques, historic buildings, and luxury high-rises, this area is known today as a great area to stay and find some of the best food and drink establishments in all of San Diego. On this fine wine tour, you will visit 3 establishments and sample a variety of premium wines from Southern California wine country and beyond. You will meet local wine enthusiasts who are eager to share with you small production wines, as well as Italian favorites. Spend the day learning about the different styles of great wine, and taste your way through Little Italy. Taste not just great wine but Italian foodie favorites such as Italian cheeses and a favorite pasta dish. A walking history tour of San Diego's own Little Italy Explore the sights, sounds, smells and flavors of this chic Downtown neighborhood Learn about unique premium wines from California, Italy and other parts of the world Get to know some of San Diego's biggest wine fanatics at 3 venues Important Information: All guests must bring valid ID with them for the wine tour. Days/Times Available*: Friday through Sunday at 3:00pm Rate (Age 21+): $125 per Person Duration: 3 Hours Minimum Guests: 4 / Maximum Guests: 12

La Jolla is known as the "Jewel of San Diego" with some much marine wildlife, art, and historic figures who have paved the way in the world of science and medicine. Learn about their stories, enjoy seaside views, and walk along the village streets lined with galleries and trendy restaurants. What to Expect: A guided walking tour of La Jolla Village & Cove, weaving together scenic oceanfront views, local history, culinary tastings, and art. The walk covers roughly ½ to ¾ mile, including a downhill segment to reach the Cove—so comfy shoes are a must. Culinary & Culinary Highlights: Enjoy a savory food and drink tasting at a local restaurant, often featuring gourmet tacos and margaritas—Puesto is one favored stop. Wrap up on a sweet note with dessert, typically artisanal gelato from a local French/Italian-style bakery. Cultural and Scenic Stops: Visit an art gallery, led by a curator, offering a glimpse into the local contemporary art scene. Stroll past iconic landmarks like Sunny Jim's Sea Cave, a quirky and historic cave accessed via the famous Cave Store. At the picturesque La Jolla Cove, spot sea lions basking on rocks and take in sweeping ocean views. Storytelling & Local Flavor: Guides share engaging stories about La Jolla's history, with a special nod to pioneering figures—especially women—who helped shape the area's rich cultural and scientific landscape. You'll also hear fun local anecdotes—about Hollywood-era hangouts, marine mysteries, and more.

title: Electric Bike/Train Coastal Tour
description: Want to ride the rails and explore on your electric or road bike? The tour begins at the Solana Beach Train Station where you will board the train, with your bike, headed to Carlsbad Village. The train fare of $5 is included in your tour charge. The train follows the coastline, so you can enjoy spectacular views along the way. Once off the train in Carlsbad, your bike adventure will begin. As you ride back to Solana Beach you will enjoy incredible coastline vistas, white sand beaches, meditation gardens, local history, and restaurants to experience for lunch. The bike riding leg of the tour is about 16 miles with up to 14 stops along the way. This tour is a cell phone, narrated and guided tour app. The map on your phone will show you the stops and the route we recommend. It will also show your position along the route, so you won't get lost. When you get close to a scheduled stop, your app tour guide will share information, history, and stories with you. Do not forget your ear buds or earphones for the best audio enjoyment. If you need to rent an electric bike for the tour please contact Pedego Solana Beach at 858.764.2979 or you can make an online reservation at pedegosolanabeach.com. Their rental bikes come with a cell phone holder on the handlebar and a standard USB port to keep your phone charged from the bike's power source. Bring your phone charging cord to connect with the bike. The bike rental is not included in the tour cost. This experience should not be rushed, so plan to start in the morning and enjoy the entire day. Fun train ride, incredible coastline views, tasty food, no traffic or parking hassles, and fun in the sun. Most of the tour you will be riding in a bike lane on a road with automobiles.

title: Cin! Cin! (Cheers!): Wine Tour
description: A guided tour through the flavors and aromas of Little Italy. Planning a romantic date or your next friend's night out? Our Little Italy wine tasting tour is the perfect way to elevate your outing and get to know one of San Diego's most unique downtown neighborhoods. As we stroll through this historic fishing town, you'll learn all about the area's rich history and, of course, sample the food and award winning wine that makes Little Italy one of the best dining locations in all of San Diego and, dare we say, Southern California. About Wine Tasting In Little ItalyVino! Vino! is a walking San Diego wine tour and a great way to get to know Little Italy, San Diego - one of the chicest neighborhoods in the Downtown area. Meet with your tour guide and head on to your culinary flavor adventure. With many unique boutiques, historic buildings, and luxury high-rises, this area is known today as a great area to stay and find some of the best food and drink establishments in all of San Diego. On this fine wine tour, you will visit 3 establishments and sample a variety of premium wines from Southern California wine country and beyond. You will meet local wine enthusiasts who are eager to share with you small production wines, as well as Italian favorites. Spend the day learning about the different styles of great wine, and taste your way through Little Italy. Taste not just great wine but Italian foodie favorites such as Italian cheeses and a favorite pasta dish. A walking history tour of San Diego's own Little Italy Explore the sights, sounds, smells and flavors of this chic Downtown neighborhood Learn about unique premium wines from California, Italy and other parts of the world Get to know some of San Diego's biggest wine fanatics at 3 venues Important Information: All guests must bring valid ID with them for the wine tour. Days/Times Available*: Friday through Sunday at 3:00pm Rate (Age 21+): $125 per Person Duration: 3 Hours Minimum Guests: 4 / Maximum Guests: 12

title: Mission Bay Parade of Lights
description: Boatloads of Family Fun on Mission Bay December 13, 2025 | 5:30PM This festive boat parade on beautiful Mission Bay takes place annually each December. Mission Bay Yacht Club has organized and hosted the holiday boat parade of lights for decades, now a beloved event that delights viewers of all ages with creativity and effort that goes into decorating a brilliant floating light display. Kick off this holiday tradition with SeaWorld's spectacular fireworks and enjoy the festive holiday boats parade on the bay from designated spectator viewing areas along the parade route. It's a night of magic, merriment, and cherished memories for the whole family. Looking to keep the holiday fun going all weekend long? Book a stay at one of our incredible resorts on the bay and make it a truly memorable family holiday weekend. SPECTATOR VIEWING AREAS Mission Bay is getting festive with family fun spectator viewing areas along the parade route. Enjoy the best views of the decorated boats while you enjoy holiday themed Mission Bay hospitality, including food & beverage service, holiday music, bonfire seating, entertainment for kids, and even a visit from Santa! The Dana on Mission Bay Winter Wonderland & Parade Viewing Party4PM - 6:30PM Experience the magic of the holidays at The Dana on Mission Bay with a festive evening of fun, food, and sparkling bay views. The Winter Wonderland celebration sets the stage for the Mission Bay Parade of Lights, featuring lively music, and themed food and drink specials. Plus, special appearances from beloved holiday characters like Princess Elsa, Buddy the Elf, and The Grinch from 4PM - 5:30PM. After the parade, stick around for the Falling Snow Party — a magical finale to a night of cheer! Upgrade your evening with VIP Parade Viewing, which includes heated seating for up to four guests, four chairs, cocktail table, unlimited hot apple cider and cocoa, and a prime spot to enjoy the dazzling boat parade. VIP SEATING TICKETS Skip the stress and reserve on-site parking in advance to stay just steps away from the celebration. Want to make a weekend of it? Book The Dana's exclusive Parade of Lights Room Package and save 15%, with rate code PARADE plus enjoy a special treat — your choice of two hot drinks and cookies or two hot festive cocktails for extra holiday cheer while watching the parade. Catamaran Resort Hotel & Spa Parade Viewing PartyNorth Lawn | 4PM - 9PM Celebrate the season at the Catamaran Resort Hotel & Spa with a festive holiday gathering on the North Lawn. Enjoy a cozy outdoor boozy hot chocolate bar featuring hot cider, mulled wine, coffee, and a selection of liqueurs. Light bites, including sandwiches, snacks, and sweet treats, will also be available. Bring your own blankets and chairs and gather with friends and family to sip, savor, and soak in the holiday spirit by the bay. Tidal at Paradise Point Resort Parade of Lights Dinner ExperienceSaturday, December 13| Parade begins at 5:30 PM Experience one of San Diego's most magical holiday traditions from the best seat on the bay. Join us at Tidal for an unforgettable Parade of Lights Dinner, featuring spectacular waterfront views as illuminated boats drift across Mission Bay. Your evening begins with a glass of champagne or a signature welcome cocktail, followed by a seasonal three-course pre-fixe menu thoughtfully curated by Chef Charlotte Guerrero. Savor the flavors of the season, sip, and celebrate as the parade lights glide across the water. Reservations required. $100 per person, includes service charge and tax. SeaWorld San Diego Want to keep the holiday fun going all weekend? Bring your family to experience the dazzling spectacles, heartwarming traditions, and joyful surprises of SeaWorld San Diego's Christmas Celebration, running daily from November 14 through January 4 and included with park admission. More details here! Mission Bay Beach Club Experience holiday magic by the bay at Mission Bay Beach Club. From the Holiday Market and Santa meet-and-greet to festive drinks, sparkling décor, and live music, it's the perfect pre-Parade destination for family fun and seasonal cheer. Holiday Market: 9 AM - 5 PM Meet Santa: 3 PM - 5 PM Live music: 2 PM - 5 PM (pending jazz band)

The tour begins at the Solana Beach Train Station where you will board the train, with your bike, headed to Carlsbad Village. The train fare of $5 is included in your tour charge. The train follows the coastline, so you can enjoy spectacular views along the way. Once off the train in Carlsbad, your bike adventure will begin. As you ride back to Solana Beach you will enjoy incredible coastline vistas, white sand beaches, meditation gardens, local history, and restaurants to experience for lunch. The bike riding leg of the tour is about 16 miles with up to 14 stops along the way. This tour is a cell phone, narrated and guided tour app. The map on your phone will show you the stops and the route we recommend. It will also show your position along the route, so you won't get lost. When you get close to a scheduled stop, your app tour guide will share information, history, and stories with you. Do not forget your ear buds or earphones for the best audio enjoyment. If you need to rent an electric bike for the tour please contact Pedego Solana Beach at 858.764.2979 or you can make an online reservation at pedegosolanabeach.com. Their rental bikes come with a cell phone holder on the handlebar and a standard USB port to keep your phone charged from the bike's power source. Bring your phone charging cord to connect with the bike. The bike rental is not included in the tour cost. This experience should not be rushed, so plan to start in the morning and enjoy the entire day. Fun train ride, incredible coastline views, tasty food, no traffic or parking hassles, and fun in the sun. Most of the tour you will be riding in a bike lane on a road with automobiles.

title: La Jolla Tour - Sip, Savor and Sea
description: The Sip, Savor and Sea tour is a great way to explore the coastal town of La Jolla with a professional tour guide. La Jolla is known as the "Jewel of San Diego" with some much marine wildlife, art, and historic figures who have paved the way in the world of science and medicine. Learn about their stories, enjoy seaside views, and walk along the village streets lined with galleries and trendy restaurants. What to Expect: A guided walking tour of La Jolla Village & Cove, weaving together scenic oceanfront views, local history, culinary tastings, and art. The walk covers roughly ½ to ¾ mile, including a downhill segment to reach the Cove—so comfy shoes are a must. Culinary & Culinary Highlights: Enjoy a savory food and drink tasting at a local restaurant, often featuring gourmet tacos and margaritas—Puesto is one favored stop. Wrap up on a sweet note with dessert, typically artisanal gelato from a local French/Italian-style bakery. Cultural and Scenic Stops: Visit an art gallery, led by a curator, offering a glimpse into the local contemporary art scene. Stroll past iconic landmarks like Sunny Jim's Sea Cave, a quirky and historic cave accessed via the famous Cave Store. At the picturesque La Jolla Cove, spot sea lions basking on rocks and take in sweeping ocean views. Storytelling & Local Flavor: Guides share engaging stories about La Jolla's history, with a special nod to pioneering figures—especially women—who helped shape the area's rich cultural and scientific landscape. You'll also hear fun local anecdotes—about Hollywood-era hangouts, marine mysteries, and more.

title: Cin! Cin! (Cheers!): Wine Tour
description: A guided tour through the flavors and aromas of Little Italy. Planning a romantic date or your next friend's night out? Our Little Italy wine tasting tour is the perfect way to elevate your outing and get to know one of San Diego's most unique downtown neighborhoods. As we stroll through this historic fishing town, you'll learn all about the area's rich history and, of course, sample the food and award winning wine that makes Little Italy one of the best dining locations in all of San Diego and, dare we say, Southern California. About Wine Tasting In Little ItalyVino! Vino! is a walking San Diego wine tour and a great way to get to know Little Italy, San Diego - one of the chicest neighborhoods in the Downtown area. Meet with your tour guide and head on to your culinary flavor adventure. With many unique boutiques, historic buildings, and luxury high-rises, this area is known today as a great area to stay and find some of the best food and drink establishments in all of San Diego. On this fine wine tour, you will visit 3 establishments and sample a variety of premium wines from Southern California wine country and beyond. You will meet local wine enthusiasts who are eager to share with you small production wines, as well as Italian favorites. Spend the day learning about the different styles of great wine, and taste your way through Little Italy. Taste not just great wine but Italian foodie favorites such as Italian cheeses and a favorite pasta dish. A walking history tour of San Diego's own Little Italy Explore the sights, sounds, smells and flavors of this chic Downtown neighborhood Learn about unique premium wines from California, Italy and other parts of the world Get to know some of San Diego's biggest wine fanatics at 3 venues Important Information: All guests must bring valid ID with them for the wine tour. Days/Times Available*: Friday through Sunday at 3:00pm Rate (Age 21+): $125 per Person Duration: 3 Hours Minimum Guests: 4 / Maximum Guests: 12

title: Cin! Cin! (Cheers!): Wine Tour
description: A guided tour through the flavors and aromas of Little Italy. Planning a romantic date or your next friend's night out? Our Little Italy wine tasting tour is the perfect way to elevate your outing and get to know one of San Diego's most unique downtown neighborhoods. As we stroll through this historic fishing town, you'll learn all about the area's rich history and, of course, sample the food and award winning wine that makes Little Italy one of the best dining locations in all of San Diego and, dare we say, Southern California. About Wine Tasting In Little ItalyVino! Vino! is a walking San Diego wine tour and a great way to get to know Little Italy, San Diego - one of the chicest neighborhoods in the Downtown area. Meet with your tour guide and head on to your culinary flavor adventure. With many unique boutiques, historic buildings, and luxury high-rises, this area is known today as a great area to stay and find some of the best food and drink establishments in all of San Diego. On this fine wine tour, you will visit 3 establishments and sample a variety of premium wines from Southern California wine country and beyond. You will meet local wine enthusiasts who are eager to share with you small production wines, as well as Italian favorites. Spend the day learning about the different styles of great wine, and taste your way through Little Italy. Taste not just great wine but Italian foodie favorites such as Italian cheeses and a favorite pasta dish. A walking history tour of San Diego's own Little Italy Explore the sights, sounds, smells and flavors of this chic Downtown neighborhood Learn about unique premium wines from California, Italy and other parts of the world Get to know some of San Diego's biggest wine fanatics at 3 venues Important Information: All guests must bring valid ID with them for the wine tour. Days/Times Available*: Friday through Sunday at 3:00pm Rate (Age 21+): $125 per Person Duration: 3 Hours Minimum Guests: 4 / Maximum Guests: 12

title: La Jolla Tour - Sip, Savor and Sea
description: The Sip, Savor and Sea tour is a great way to explore the coastal town of La Jolla with a professional tour guide. La Jolla is known as the "Jewel of San Diego" with some much marine wildlife, art, and historic figures who have paved the way in the world of science and medicine. Learn about their stories, enjoy seaside views, and walk along the village streets lined with galleries and trendy restaurants. What to Expect: A guided walking tour of La Jolla Village & Cove, weaving together scenic oceanfront views, local history, culinary tastings, and art. The walk covers roughly ½ to ¾ mile, including a downhill segment to reach the Cove—so comfy shoes are a must. Culinary & Culinary Highlights: Enjoy a savory food and drink tasting at a local restaurant, often featuring gourmet tacos and margaritas—Puesto is one favored stop. Wrap up on a sweet note with dessert, typically artisanal gelato from a local French/Italian-style bakery. Cultural and Scenic Stops: Visit an art gallery, led by a curator, offering a glimpse into the local contemporary art scene. Stroll past iconic landmarks like Sunny Jim's Sea Cave, a quirky and historic cave accessed via the famous Cave Store. At the picturesque La Jolla Cove, spot sea lions basking on rocks and take in sweeping ocean views. Storytelling & Local Flavor: Guides share engaging stories about La Jolla's history, with a special nod to pioneering figures—especially women—who helped shape the area's rich cultural and scientific landscape. You'll also hear fun local anecdotes—about Hollywood-era hangouts, marine mysteries, and more.

description: Join us every Thursday at 7pm for Trivia Night at Union Gaslamp, where you can put your knowledge to the test while enjoying an evening of fun, camaraderie, and great drinks.

title: Electric Bike/Train Coastal Tour
description: Want to ride the rails and explore on your electric or road bike? The tour begins at the Solana Beach Train Station where you will board the train, with your bike, headed to Carlsbad Village. The train fare of $5 is included in your tour charge. The train follows the coastline, so you can enjoy spectacular views along the way. Once off the train in Carlsbad, your bike adventure will begin. As you ride back to Solana Beach you will enjoy incredible coastline vistas, white sand beaches, meditation gardens, local history, and restaurants to experience for lunch. The bike riding leg of the tour is about 16 miles with up to 14 stops along the way. This tour is a cell phone, narrated and guided tour app. The map on your phone will show you the stops and the route we recommend. It will also show your position along the route, so you won't get lost. When you get close to a scheduled stop, your app tour guide will share information, history, and stories with you. Do not forget your ear buds or earphones for the best audio enjoyment. Fun train ride, incredible coastline views, tasty food, no traffic or parking hassles, and fun in the sun. Most of the tour you will be riding in a bike lane on a road with automobiles.

title: Cin! Cin! (Cheers!): Wine Tour
description: A guided tour through the flavors and aromas of Little Italy. Planning a romantic date or your next friend's night out? Our Little Italy wine tasting tour is the perfect way to elevate your outing and get to know one of San Diego's most unique downtown neighborhoods. As we stroll through this historic fishing town, you'll learn all about the area's rich history and, of course, sample the food and award winning wine that makes Little Italy one of the best dining locations in all of San Diego and, dare we say, Southern California. About Wine Tasting In Little ItalyVino! Vino! is a walking San Diego wine tour and a great way to get to know Little Italy, San Diego - one of the chicest neighborhoods in the Downtown area. Meet with your tour guide and head on to your culinary flavor adventure. With many unique boutiques, historic buildings, and luxury high-rises, this area is known today as a great area to stay and find some of the best food and drink establishments in all of San Diego. On this fine wine tour, you will visit 3 establishments and sample a variety of premium wines from Southern California wine country and beyond. You will meet local wine enthusiasts who are eager to share with you small production wines, as well as Italian favorites. Spend the day learning about the different styles of great wine, and taste your way through Little Italy. Taste not just great wine but Italian foodie favorites such as Italian cheeses and a favorite pasta dish. A walking history tour of San Diego's own Little Italy Explore the sights, sounds, smells and flavors of this chic Downtown neighborhood Learn about unique premium wines from California, Italy and other parts of the world Get to know some of San Diego's biggest wine fanatics at 3 venues Important Information: All guests must bring valid ID with them for the wine tour. Days/Times Available*: Friday through Sunday at 3:00pm Rate (Age 21+): $125 per Person Duration: 3 Hours Minimum Guests: 4 / Maximum Guests: 12
